name: The Crooked Constable
description: A Gaff letter found in the dungeon beneath Droomen Knoll revealed that Constable Sidney Wolder is an agent of the Nag-Lord — the same entity responsible for creating the crookhorns. Wolder has been operating inside Lankshorn's constabulary. The party knows, and Wolder does not know they know.
objectives: Determine the full extent of the Nag-Lord's network in Lankshorn.
Decide what to do with knowledge of Wolder's treachery.
Determine whether Sgt. Marge Bregson is an ally or another agent.
leads: The Gaff letter, translated by Llerg, explicitly named Wolder as a Nag-Lord contact. Llerg was bribed to keep silent and has since left Lankshorn for High Hankle.
Jack sent a false letter to the constabulary claiming the party would depart on Moot — a day later than planned — hoping to forestall any ambush on the road.
Brennan was assigned by Sgt. Bregson to accompany the party as a representative of the town guard. His loyalties remain unclear.
Sgt. Marge Bregson's allegiance is uncertain. She may be a genuine ally or part of the same network.

Pro-Ramius breggles — Marialla, James, and Fisk — roughed up Llerg at the Bogways in Session 3, suggesting an organized faction hostile to Malbleat's interests and likely connected to the broader crookhorn conspiracy.
